title: cyrtomium auriculatum
intro: Cyrtomium auriculatum is a species of fern commonly known as the ear-leafed fern, belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Asian cultures, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Cyrtomium auriculatum include its ability to aid in the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. In terms of herbal preparations, Cyrtomium auriculatum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can be dried or frozen for later use in traditional medicine.
